All right, welcome to the explanation of the deliverables. So these are going to be the things that you are giving to your clients when they get to the gym for nutrition orientation or your bi-weekly check-ins. So they are basically the meal plans, right? So to find them, we are going to go into, we're in fulfillment right now, we're gonna go to fulfillment downloads and you are gonna click on fulfillment downloads. So once you click on that, it is going to take you into the lesson where it has the downloadable resources, the first one being the food list. So week zero to two, they're just eating off the food list. I'm not gonna explain how that works, that's all in the nutrition explanation, but this is where it is located, the food list. So what you will do is you will click on this button and it is going to download it to your computer once it loads. All right, so there it is on the bottom and to open it up, you just click on it, it will come in a PDF form, it is unbranded, you can put your logo on it if you would like, you can send these to your clients, you can print them off, they do have white backgrounds, so they don't take up a lot of ink if you do print them off and they look really clean and super simple to follow. So what you'll wanna do to be able to print this is once it loads fully, it's not fully loaded yet, it'll give you a little arrow that you can click on and that will download it to your computer off of our website and then you can print it and send it and do whatever you'd like. So let's go through, while it's loading, we'll go through what it looks like. So the first one is very, very simple, it says basically eat only these foods, right? So they can eat the foods off of the approved list, so proteins, fats, carbs and veggies, there's no portion tracking or anything like this in week zero to two. So you will print this off and you will give this to your clients on Nutrition Orientation Day and it's what they'll follow the first two weeks until they come in for their first bi-weekly check-in. So super simple, there's an area to write notes, if they have any food allergies or anything specific that you wanna write down that you guys discuss in the Nutrition Orientation, that's where you'll wanna annotate it. Then we have the supplements, so you'll want to mark off which supplements they're taking, whether it's burn AM and test storm in the morning or sleep multiplier and you could write in whatever you want as well and what you recommend. And then the pre and post and during workouts supplements. So whether it's pre-intra protein, that is all included on the bottom. So very simple one-page document that you can just print off or send them a link to. Now what we'll do is we'll go back and here you can see the hybrid food list with portion sizes, the meal plans 1.25, through 2.75, so that is all you'll be using. So we can click on, I have each one of these downloaded already so we don't have to keep downloading them. This is the first one and here is the little arrow on the top when it fully loads and to click that or to download it to your computer you just click this and it'll bring up where do you wanna download this to, you pick where you want it to go and click save. Okay, so that is the food list. The next one that you can take a look at is going to be the food list that has portion sizes but this is just eyeball measuring it using their hand as a reference. So we haven't quite gotten into using the scale and measuring on a scale yet but that is where we're headed. So the first one is just measuring based on palm, thumb, fist or fist and that is for protein, fats, carbs and veggies and then so they're just eyeballing and then they're roughly sticking to the portion that they can have per meal. So for breakfast it's a carb, a protein and a vegetable and they just pick anything off of this list and just have one, one and one of those based on their palm size, thumb size, fist size and so on. Then they'll have a half portion of protein so they can literally have half of a palm size or they can have like a protein shake which is typically gonna be half the amount of crams or ounces of a full serving of protein. Same thing for lunch and then for dinner is where we have the fat and we do not have a carb for dinner and again they are just doing rough estimates and underneath we have the supplements. So similar if you want to change up their supplements or their supplement routine at the bi-weekly so this is the meal plan they should be getting on when they come in week two. After two completed weeks this is what we are going to change it to so they're sticking to a little bit smaller portions than they were probably eating week zero to week two. The next plan is where we get into the meal plans 1.25 through 2.75 and so those are based on their weight. So if they weigh 100 pounds to 150 pounds they should be following the 1.25. If they weigh 150 pounds to 200 pounds they should be following the 1.75. If they weigh 200 to 250 pounds they should be following the 2.25 and if they weigh 250 plus they should be following the 2.75 meal plans. Okay and the difference between them is the portion sizes right here. So this says six ounces, six ounces, six ounces of protein, two tablespoons of facts or depending on what the fat is might be measured differently. Carbs, it'll say three quarters of a cup or a slice and a half of Ezekiel bread and vegetables is the same. But that's where we manipulate the actual size. So this is when you're recommending that they use a food scale. So it's not, so they're following a very similar approach that they had week two to week four except now they're weighing everything on the scale and that is going to get them one step closer to being able to track macros and things like that. So they're going to be measuring this and then eating it. Okay, so this is the food list that comes with their plan depending on what weight they are and very similar. We have the single portions below because you don't need to do any math, they just need to eat as much as what is above. And then for dinner there's zero. For fat, if they're following the 1.25 and we'll go through the other ones too so you can see how they're different. But if they're following the 1.25 and Dr. Cashew will explain this much more than I am, I'm just explaining the sheets. But the idea behind it is they shouldn't need any additional fats because they're already getting fats from the other foods that they're eating. And then the protein is on the bottom. We always have the protein in case you want to change their, I'm sorry, the supplements are on the bottom. We always have the supplements down there in case you want to change their routine throughout the plan. You'll notice as you scroll down there is a second page to these plans and it's a grocery helper. And this is just so since they are eating specific amounts of food, we can help them with buying enough for the week. So that's what this does. As an example, if they write in for a breakfast that they want to have and they look at their list and they want to have eggs or something like that, we know that they'll need to pick up one 24 ounce egg whites, right? A container of egg whites. So they can write their protein down here under breakfast as egg whites. And when they get to the grocery store, they might have that thing circled and they can just get in and out with exactly how much they need. That way they don't go in and they buy 12 ounces and then halfway through the week, they run out and they're being like, I had to have a cheeseburger because I was out of eggs. Like we want to get rid of excuses for them. So fats and carbs the same way, they're just picking the meals or picking the ingredients that they want to have for those meals and selecting the bulk amount above that will last them for the entire week in this grocery helper. So again, at the bottom, there is going to be options for them to have zero calorie beverages, spices and condiments, right? So mustard, soy sauce, hot sauces, all of that kind of stuff. And spices, they can have anything listed down here. These are all just flavor enhancers, right? So this is what the 1.25 grocery plan and meal food list looks like going to the 2.75, I'm sorry, the 1.75. It is very similar, except they do have that added portion of fat for their dinner. So everything else remains the same. Everything else will be, yep, it is the same. Then moving to the 2.25, we have an increased portion of carbs, so it's double the size of carbs and that will be added to their breakfast and lunch, not added, but that's how much more they get compared to somebody eating the 1.75. So that is the difference between those two. And then when you get to the 2.75, everything remains the same, except they have an increased portion of protein. So now they have the increased carbs and then they'll have the increased protein from six to nine ounces of protein. And that's just the sustain the muscle mass that they probably have weighing as much as they do to make sure that they are at the proper ratios. And this is setting them up to be able to eat much more effectively when they get farther down in the plan. And after these meal plans, these templated meal plans, we'll be able to start creating custom meal plans for them where we just have them track calories in my fitness paler or something like that, along with macros, and they'll be, it won't be that big of a shift for them to hit the macro goals that you give them because they've been used to eating about that volume of protein, carbs and fats from these meal plans when they first started. So this is all the downloadable resources that you'll find in here. Like I said, I recommend probably printing off at least the first one. So when they come to nutrition orientation, they have that piece of paper in their hand and they have the game plan. It's up to you if you wanna continue printing these other ones, they are only two pages. We try to make them printer friendly by making the backgrounds white, but you could also send them the links as well. So that covers the downloadable resources. And now we can head to the next part of the training.
